content: The intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) that drives autonomous agent exploration can be reframed as a dynamic pruning gate for 2025 SOTA active learning agent memory, only allocating long-term memory storage to unexpected, learnable state transitions that trigger high intrinsic reward. This creates a self-organizing memory system that automatically prioritizes storing data that will improve the agent's generalization and future exploration, rather than relying on manual uncertainty sampling heuristics.
